title: erigeron alpinus subsp. pyrenaicus
intro: Erigeron alpinus subsp. pyrenaicus, also known as the Pyrenean Fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Pyrenees mountains in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Erigeron alpinus subsp. pyrenaicus have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support skin health, reduce pain and inflammation, and boost the immune system.
